original_hypothesis.title: Tokenomics epoch settlement can be re-entered via Treasury.rebalanceTreasury to corrupt epochCounter and incentive accounting
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Tokenomics.checkpoint() makes an external call to ITreasury(treasury).rebalanceTreasury(...) without a reentrancy guard. A malicious or compromised Treasury could re-enter Tokenomics.checkpoint() during settlement, potentially causing double-settlement-like state corruption (e.g., epochCounter, effectiveBond, maxBond, mapEpochStakingPoints) in ways that violate epoch monotonicity and break invariants across epochs.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Phase A explicitly flags absence of reentrancy guard in Tokenomics.checkpoint despite external call. While treasury is typically trusted, this becomes a real operational threat if treasury is upgraded, replaced, or compromised.
deployment_assumptions: Treasury is upgradeable or can be replaced via Tokenomics.changeManagers
Treasury.rebalanceTreasury can call back into Tokenomics.checkpoint (directly or indirectly)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Tokenomics.checkpoint calls ITreasury(treasury).rebalanceTreasury(...); Tokenomics has changeManagers allowing treasury replacement (Tokenomics.sol: changeManagers). No treasury code provided to confirm reentrancy behavior.

proof_of_signal.pattern: External call to ITreasury(treasury).rebalanceTreasury(...) near end of checkpoint; no _locked check used in checkpoint

preconditions: Treasury.rebalanceTreasury can perform re-entrant call into Tokenomics.checkpoint within same transaction
Epoch time conditions still pass on re-entry (diffNumSeconds >= epochLen)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Checkpoint is permissionless and performs multiple state updates before and after an external call.
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Treasury address is owner-controlled; threat manifests if governance misconfigures treasury or treasury becomes compromised.

operational_questions: Is Treasury upgradeable or can it be swapped quickly by governance?
Does Treasury.rebalanceTreasury have any callback capability to Tokenomics?
Are there off-chain monitors preventing unexpected treasury changes?
validation_requirements: Model a malicious treasury that re-enters checkpoint and observe state corruption
Check whether partial state updates before the external call can be exploited to create inconsistent effectiveBond/maxBond/epochCounter

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Set or compromise Treasury to a contract that, when called, calls Tokenomics.checkpoint() again.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger Tokenomics.checkpoint() once; during rebalanceTreasury, re-enter checkpoint.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Observe whether epochCounter increments incorrectly or per-epoch points are overwritten, enabling later reward misallocation.

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Potential full tokenomics accounting corruption; may require governance intervention or migration.
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Epoch settlement and inflation schedule become unreliable; downstream incentives can be miscomputed.
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High: repairing epoch history on-chain is difficult.
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All epoch-based rewards, staking incentives, bond limits

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: None if treasury compromised; otherwise requires governance control to set malicious treasury.

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Unexpected treasury address changes; unusual nested calls during checkpoint transactions.
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: EpochSettled events emitted multiple times in a single tx or inconsistent epochCounter progression.
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Treasury contracts with callbacks into tokenomics.

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
